events.label: reasoning
events.text: Exit sign dead battery during test-button check. In-service. Emergency egress lighting must function. Dead battery = failed component, non-compliant with NEC 700 and NFPA 101. Decision: fail. Risk: high (life safety).

    "Exit sign emergency battery failed test-button check; unit will not illuminate on loss of normal power.",
    "Dead battery in in-service exit sign violates emergency egress lighting requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace failed battery immediately and retest unit to confirm 90-minute emergency operation.",
    "Inspect all similar exit/emergency egress units for battery failure and log results.",
    "Verify charging circuit and line-voltage supply to the exit sign after battery replacement."

  "rationale": "An exit sign with a dead battery cannot provide required illumination upon loss of normal power. NEC 700 and NFPA 101 require emergency egress lighting to be maintained in operable condition and tested periodically; a failed test-button result indicates the emergency function is non-functional, making the installation non-compliant and a life-safety hazard.",
